edges.note: Both are baseline accessibility obligations covering the same email scope.
Flush stacking with no margins requires each section to declare edge backgrounds for match/transition.
QA verifies the theme consistency the edge-declaration rule requires.
Both constrain what sections may add across the assembled email.
Title is purple; all other headings/body take their default colors, and none may be purple.
Both forbid purple/green in body; highlight rule specifies black+bold as the sanctioned emphasis.
No purple anywhere except the title, so body highlights fall back to black bold.
Scene-color pairings and their fixed callout opacities apply together in campaign callouts.
The email baseline constrains the general gradient usage: no gradient text in email, requiring solid fallback and split-headline technique instead.
Approved tiles are the values selected by the single per-email theme.
Contrast pairings specify text/link treatments for the theme backgrounds.
Constrains the single green panel that appears in the sequence.
Edge-to-edge butting realizes the sequenced background transitions.
Image variant always lands on a dark-group background, so button/text follow the dark styling.
Adds LPGA gating and teal reservation to the general image/background pairing rule.
When a populated section is directly above, the CTA runs image-free, bypassing the image/background pairing.
The fluid/hybrid build technique implements the 600/375 canvas widths.
The no-margin/edge-to-edge rule refines the baseline spacing system's goal of clean section butting.
Both are banner-callout opacity treatments over backgrounds.
Integrity/no-substitution reinforces the fixed composition and order of the trio.
Placement/layout accompanies the composition rule within the same symptom-trio section.
CTA button left-flush placement is a section-specific refinement of the global left-alignment rule.
Substitutes Arial for Avenir weights when the surface is email or PowerPoint.
Constrains the Heavy weight referenced in the ISI boxed-warning hierarchy row to that use only.
Enforcement hard rules protecting the fixed section-padding system.
ISI/footer sizes in the type scale belong to the locked build, not editorial defaults.
Campaign FLIP THE SCRIPT headlines use ALL CAPS instead of the default Title Case.
Display lockup treatment applies alongside the ALL CAPS campaign headline casing.
The prohibited-uses list constrains the same bold-usage topic defined by the reserved-targets rule.
Both govern emphasis usage (bold/italic) in email body copy.
Locked matter is the bracketing pair in the mandatory assembly order.
Restricts which optional components may appear and forbids adding new ones.
Edge-join behavior depends on the locked top/end matter positions in the sequence.
Both are LOCKED supplied matter with inherited consequences for content sections.
Content-section body size/spacing inherits the locked footer ISI 12px/1.4 setting.
Sibling primary component options in the same template library.
Sibling component specs in the same template library.
Voice framing and symptom terminology jointly govern DTP patient copy.
Excludes the HCP default label from the DTP label conventions.
Poll response button is a special case of an approved DTP CTA label styled as primary.
